obligations.reason: A visible zeroriscy_debug_unit and core debug/control modules indicate debug paths may reach protected CPU state; the check must determine whether authorization guards cover all debug operations rather than assuming debug logic is safe or unsafe.
obligations.subject: debug interface / debug unit
obligations.operation: read/write/override/trigger debug control of core state
obligations.object: core architectural state, debug control state, register file, PC/control-flow state
obligations.expected_guard: debug entry and debug operations should be mediated by explicit debug authorization/authentication or a trusted debug-mode state before protected core state is exposed or modified
state_conditions_to_check: debug request while core is running
required_evidence_slots: target core state or control object
reachable path from debug unit to object
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, path/reachability
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated and does not list all debug ports; inspect RTL port connections and signal names to identify exact debug request and authorization signals.

obligations.reason: The scope contains a dedicated CSR register module with multiple decode cases; visible RTL should be checked for whether software CSR operations are permission-checked at decode and update sites.
obligations.subject: CPU software executing CSR instructions
obligations.object: control/status registers and security-relevant CPU state
obligations.expected_guard: CSR reads and writes that affect privilege/security state, exception/interrupt state, or debug state should be gated by legal CSR address, access type, current privilege/debug mode, and read-only/write permissions
obligations.candidate_path: zeroriscy_decoder/id_stage/cs_registers CSR decode and writeback path
obligations.uncertainty: Zero-riscy may implement a limited privilege model; determine expected guard from implemented CSR access attributes and mode signals, not from names alone.

obligations.reason: fc_demux instances route core instruction and data requests based on decode cases; permission analysis should ensure address routing preserves intended memory/peripheral access policy and does not create unguarded aliases.
obligations.subject: fabric controller core instruction/data bus master
obligations.object: SoC address regions, TCDM/peripheral access paths, instruction/data bus targets
obligations.expected_guard: address decode and security/privilege attributes should consistently restrict instruction and data accesses to protected regions or peripherals, with no alternate demux output bypassing the intended range or mode check
obligations.candidate_path: fc_subsystem core data/instruction buses -> fc_demux -> external/peripheral/TCDM buses
obligations.uncertainty: Protected regions are inferred from address-map and security-mode signals; inspect include definitions for concrete ranges and security attributes.

obligations.reason: A visible load/store unit generates data bus transactions; check whether it or downstream fabric enforces memory-region permissions for protected MMIO or memory regions.
obligations.subject: CPU load/store unit
obligations.object: data memory and memory-mapped protected regions
obligations.expected_guard: load and store requests to protected address regions should be mediated by address-range/PMP-like checks, privilege/security mode, and operation type before issuing bus requests
obligations.uncertainty: The presence of PMP is not established in the fact layer; if absent, document where protection is expected from SoC fabric rather than CPU.

obligations.reason: The interrupt controller exposes security-relevant outputs including core_irq_sec_o, fetch_en_o, and clock enable; visible RTL should show whether APB/configuration writes and event inputs are mediated by secure-mode or register protections.
obligations.subject: APB/MMIO software or event source
obligations.object: interrupt controller configuration, secure interrupt indication, fetch enable, core clock enable
obligations.expected_guard: writes or events that alter secure interrupt attribution, fetch enable, or core clocking should be gated by authorized APB access, secure mode/lifecycle state, or lock/protection bits
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults for fetch/clock/secure interrupt state
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer ports include security-relevant signals but not all APB port details due truncation; inspect full module to identify register map and write paths.

obligations.reason: Visible interfaces include test_mode_i, BIST, bypass, cache/private and clock-gating controls; these can be legitimate but require guards to prevent normal-mode access or state exposure through test paths.
obligations.subject: test/BIST/bypass interface
obligations.object: memory/test-visible state, cache/TCDM control state, core clock/fetch control
obligations.expected_guard: test, BIST, bypass, and clock-gate controls should only affect protected memories or core operation under authorized test/lifecycle mode and should not expose or modify memory contents in normal operation
obligations.candidate_path: test_mode_i/BIST/bypass/clkgate interfaces -> memories/cache/TCDM or core controls
obligations.uncertainty: Interfaces file defines signals but may not implement policy; inspect connected modules in this scope for actual mediation or note if policy is external.

obligations.reason: Controller and IF-stage FSMs plus debug and interrupt signals determine whether protected execution state is exposed or controlled across lifecycle transitions; check reset defaults and transitions for unsafe residual permissions.
obligations.subject: core control FSM, interrupt controller, debug unit
obligations.operation: reset/mode transition/trigger fetch or debug action
obligations.object: PC/fetch state, debug state, interrupt/fetch enable state
obligations.expected_guard: reset, halt, exception, interrupt, and debug mode transitions should clear or correctly gate stale protected state and should not leave debug/fetch controls enabled without authorization
obligations.candidate_path: zeroriscy_controller/if_stage/debug_unit reset and mode-transition control -> PC/fetch/debug state
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL inspection to identify exact FSM states and whether any outputs are externally observable or security-sensitive.

obligations.reason: The scope includes an fc_hwpe module and HW accelerator-style interfaces; permission obligations should check whether accelerator-originated transactions are routed and constrained like other bus masters.
obligations.subject: HWPE / hardware accelerator master
obligations.object: memory regions reachable through HWPE/fabric interfaces
obligations.expected_guard: hardware accelerator requests should be constrained by address-range/security policy and should not gain broader memory access than the CPU or configured permissions allow
obligations.candidate_path: fc_hwpe and hw accelerator interfaces -> core/fabric memory access
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not expose all fc_hwpe ports; inspect module to determine whether it is a bus master, wrapper, or control-only block.
